objective: Plan the second narrow implementation child under issue #612, bounded to fallback-log schema/workflow parity for degraded same-family fallback evidence only.
scope_boundary: Planning-only governance child lane for issue #629.
Owns fallback-log schema/workflow parity planning only, including the existing fallback-log schema workflow gate.
Keeps execution-scope enforcement closed under #625, local-hook children closed under #615, planning-authority work under #607, and downstream verifier work under #614 out of scope.
Keeps the dirty primary checkout isolated by working only inside the issue-629 worktree.
out_of_scope: Execution-scope schema/assert/test surfaces already merged through issue #625.
Local startup, mutation, backlog, or governance-hook children already merged under issue #615.
Broader handoff-package, packet-routing, or workflow-routing enforcement.
Issue #614 downstream verifier or drift-gate work.
research_summary: Grounding intake was completed from docs/PROGRESS.md, issue #612, merged child issue #625, parent issue #615, and the current fallback-log parity surfaces. Specialist review concluded that issue #625 already closed the execution-scope side and explicitly left fallback-log schema/workflow parity open. The next narrow child should therefore own only .github/scripts/check_fallback_log_schema.py, scripts/model-fallback-log.sh, .github/workflows/check-fallback-log-schema.yml, focused tests for those surfaces, and issue-local artifacts, while leaving execution-scope, local-hook, #607, and #614 work out of scope.

sprints.name: Intake And Ownership Boundary
sprints.goal: Define why issue #629 exists and keep it narrower than the remaining issue #612 residuals.
tasks: Review docs/PROGRESS.md, issue #612, merged child issue #625, and the current fallback-log parity surfaces.
Record the exact owned fallback-log surfaces and the explicit out-of-scope execution-scope, local-hook, #607, and #614 surfaces.
deliverables: State the exact owned fallback-log surfaces.
State the explicit out-of-scope execution-scope, local-hook, #607, and #614 surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: The packet states that issue #629 is the next narrow implementation child under issue #612 after merged issue #625.

The packet names only .github/scripts/check_fallback_log_schema.py, scripts/model-fallback-log.sh, .github/workflows/check-fallback-log-schema.yml, focused tests, and issue-local artifacts as the owned implementation surface.
The packet explicitly excludes execution-scope rework, local-hook children already merged under #615, and broader validator/workflow routing work.

sprints.name: Fallback-Log Proof Contract
sprints.goal: Define the exact positive and negative proof cases for the later fallback-log parity implementation lane.
tasks: Define the required fallback-log parity fields and semantic expectations for degraded same-family fallback evidence.
List the exact positive and negative fail-closed proof cases for the later implementation lane.
Require regression proof that unchanged valid or unrelated fallback-log paths still pass.
deliverables: Define the required fallback-log parity content contract for degraded same-family fallback evidence.
State that the writer/checker reason-contract mismatch is deferred to the implementation lane and explicitly grandfather historical logs outside the PR diff.
Define the positive pass cases and the negative fail-closed cases for missing, blank, generic, semantically inconsistent, or malformed fallback-log content.
Require regression and workflow proof for unrelated or valid fallback-log flows.
acceptance_criteria: The packet names the required fallback-log parity contract by purpose.
The packet explicitly states that the concrete reason contract is deferred to the implementation lane and must be resolved there.
The packet names exact positive and negative proof cases for the later implementation lane.
The packet requires regression proof that existing valid paths still pass unchanged.

sprints.name: Cross-Family Review And Packet Closeout
sprints.goal: Run the sanctioned alternate-family review and close the planning packet cleanly.
tasks: Run the sanctioned Claude review wrapper on the issue-629 packet.
Capture the issue-scoped review artifact and fold any follow-up into the packet.
Run the planning validators and diff hygiene.
deliverables: Hand the issue-629 packet to the sanctioned Claude review wrapper path in this worktree.
Record the issue-scoped alternate-family review artifact and fold any follow-up into the packet.
Run the planning validators and issue-local diff hygiene.
acceptance_criteria: A real alternate-family review artifact exists for issue #629.
The packet validates structurally and remains planning-only.
Implementation remains blocked pending a later implementation lane.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify bounded ownership, required parity proof cases, and explicit out-of-scope surfaces for issue #629.
specialist_reviews.summary: Issue #629 should authorize only .github/scripts/check_fallback_log_schema.py, scripts/model-fallback-log.sh, .github/workflows/check-fallback-log-schema.yml, focused tests, and issue-local artifacts, while excluding execution-scope rework, local-hook children, #607, and #614.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the minimum planning packet, handoff, and parity proof contract required before issue #629 implementation can start.
specialist_reviews.summary: The planning packet needs the canonical structured plan, planning execution scope, accepted handoff, issue/branch alignment, standard packet validators, and an explicit reason-contract/grandfathering decision before implementation can start.

alternate_model_review.summary: Sanctioned alternate-family review approved the issue-629 planning packet with non-blocking follow-up to add the workflow file to blocked_paths and keep the concrete reason schema decision deferred explicitly to the implementation lane.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #629 is now promoted into the bounded implementation slice for fallback-log checker, writer, and workflow parity only, with focused tests, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the bounded fallback-log parity slice only; do not widen into execution-scope, local-hook, #607, or #614 work.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Issue #629 remains bounded to .github/scripts/check_fallback_log_schema.py, scripts/model-fallback-log.sh, .github/workflows/check-fallback-log-schema.yml, focused tests for those surfaces, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts only.
The implementation must make an explicit writer/checker reason-contract decision and keep degraded same-family fallback semantics machine-checkable.
The implementation must return positive, negative, workflow, and downstream-regression proof for the bounded fallback-log parity slice.
Issue #629 does not claim #612 closure by itself.

material_deviation_rules: Any patch to fallback-log scripts, tests, or workflows in issue #629 is a material deviation from planning-only scope.
Any edit to execution-scope schema/assert/test surfaces is a material deviation from issue #629 scope.
Any edit to local-hook children already merged under #615 is a material deviation from issue #629 scope.
Any edit to broader packet-routing or handoff enforcement surfaces is a material deviation from issue #629 scope.
Any claim that issue #629 closes issue #612 by itself is a material deviation.
